title: PROPERTY-DEPTH
scope: The property/building/unit domain is the last core entity without a Tier-7 depth pass. Today: Property is thin (name, type, address, estimated_value; TenantScope; hasMany Building; hasManyThrough Unit). Building is rich (amenities JSON {selected:[keys], custom:[strings]} gated to Building::AMENITY_OPTIONS 6 categories incl utilities/security/parking/common/unit_features/neighborhood; coordinates; photos; water billing; target_occupancy_rate; currency; wings). Building::getActiveAmenities() returns {key,label,category} but amenities are a FLAT selected list — no per-amenity detail (parking SPACES, utility PROVIDER/account, monthly cost). Multi-property: PropertyController has only index()+store() (no show); /properties redirects to /buildings; there is NO property switcher in the nav and NO persistent active property; DashboardService takes ?property_id (first if absent); Phase 74 added a building-scope (active_building|all_buildings) on the main_dashboard LandlordDashboard row. Reporting: NoiService::byProperty(landlordId) gives per-property revenue/direct+allocated expenses/NOI/noi_margin + portfolio total — the ONLY property-level aggregation; there is NO property occupancy/rent-roll metric, NO property-vs-property benchmark, NO property dashboard/show. Goals: (1) deepen amenity attributes (per-amenity quantity/provider/cost), (2) multi-property switching UX (active property + nav switcher + property show/dashboard), (3) property-level reporting + cross-property benchmarks. All work landlord-scoped via TenantScope; reuse NoiService + the Phase 50/74 report+dashboard patterns + HubShell archetype. NO DB::raw with user input (selectRaw/DB::raw static aggregates are the accepted pattern).

findings.title: Per-amenity detail (quantity / provider / account / monthly cost) — schema + model + sync service
findings.evidence: building.amenities is a flat {selected:[keys], custom:[strings]} (Building.php:380 getActiveAmenities). A caretaker/landlord can mark 'parking' but not record HOW MANY spaces, or 'wifi' without the provider/account, or any monthly cost — so amenities can't drive ops or cost reporting.
findings.remediation: NET-NEW building_amenity_details (building_id FK cascade, landlord_id FK, amenity_key string(64), quantity unsignedInteger nullable, provider string(120) nullable, account_ref string(120) nullable, monthly_cost_cents unsignedBigInteger nullable, timestamps) unique(building_id, amenity_key). BuildingAmenityDetail model (TenantScope). App\Services\Building\AmenityDetailService::sync(Building, array $details): allow-list-gated to Building::getAllAmenityKeys() AND only for currently-selected amenities (a detail for an unselected/invalid key is dropped); updateOrCreate keyed (building_id, amenity_key); removes details for amenities no longer selected. Phase78 test: sync persists details for selected keys, rejects unknown/unselected keys, prunes deselected.

findings.title: BuildingController persists amenity details + getActiveAmenities merges them + wing sync
findings.evidence: BuildingController::updateSettings only writes building.amenities; Building::getActiveAmenities returns no detail. Wing buildings share settings via BuildingService::syncSharedSettings.
findings.remediation: UpdateBuildingSettingsRequest accepts an amenity_details map (validated shape); updateSettings routes it through AmenityDetailService::sync after the amenities write. Building::getActiveAmenities() (or a sibling withDetails()) merges each active amenity with its detail row. AmenityDetailService participates in the wing-share path so a parent's amenity details propagate to wings (or are explicitly scoped per building — pick one + document). Phase78 test: updateSettings persists details; getActiveAmenities exposes them.

findings.title: Buildings/Edit amenities tab gains per-amenity detail inputs + display
findings.evidence: Buildings/Edit.vue amenities tab is checkboxes only.
findings.remediation: When an amenity is selected, reveal compact detail inputs (quantity / provider / monthly cost) bound into amenity_details; the building show page renders the detail beside each amenity. lang building.amenity_detail.*. Phase78 surface asserts the Vue tokens.

findings.title: PropertyMetricsService — per-property buildings/units/occupancy aggregation (no N+1)
findings.evidence: No service aggregates a property's portfolio. NoiService::byProperty has revenue/NOI but no occupancy or unit counts; DashboardService is building-scoped.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Property\PropertyMetricsService::forProperty(Property): {property_id, name, building_count, unit_count, occupied_count, vacant_count, occupancy_pct} + forLandlord(int): list over all the landlord's properties. Strictly landlord-scoped; counts batched via grouped queries joining units->buildings->property (NOT per-property loops). Phase78 test: counts + occupancy correct for a seeded property; another landlord's property never appears.

findings.title: Property rent-roll + arrears in the metrics
findings.evidence: Occupancy alone doesn't tell a landlord the money picture per property.
findings.remediation: Extend PropertyMetricsService with monthly_rent_roll (sum of active-lease rent across the property's occupied units) + outstanding_arrears (sum of invoice outstanding for the property's leases), batched + landlord-scoped, money in the subsystem's existing convention. Phase78 test: rent roll sums active leases; arrears sums outstanding; cross-tenant isolation.

findings.title: forLandlord powers the property index + the switcher
findings.evidence: /properties redirects to /buildings; there's no property list with metrics.
findings.remediation: PropertyMetricsService::forLandlord returns each property with its metrics for a real properties index + the nav switcher payload. Phase78 test: forLandlord returns one row per property with metrics; empty-portfolio returns [].

findings.title: PropertyController::show — single-property dashboard (owner-gated)
findings.evidence: PropertyController has no show(); a landlord cannot view one property's aggregated stats + its buildings. /properties is a redirect.
findings.remediation: PropertyController::show(Property) gated abort_unless property.landlord_id === acting landlord (404), renders Property/Show with PropertyMetricsService::forProperty + the property's buildings (each with unit/occupancy counts) + NoiService single-property slice. Route properties.show (role:landlord, whereNumber). Phase78 test: owner sees their property's metrics + buildings; another landlord gets 404.

findings.title: Property/Show.vue (HubShell archetype) + buildings drill-down
findings.remediation: Property/Show.vue using the HubShell archetype (per the design system) — KPI cards (occupancy/rent roll/arrears/NOI) + the buildings list linking to each building dashboard. data-testid='property-show'. lang property.show.*. Phase78 surface asserts the route/Vue token + lang.

findings.title: Real /properties index (metrics) instead of the buildings redirect
findings.evidence: GET /properties just redirects to /buildings, hiding the property tier.
findings.remediation: PropertyController::index renders a Properties/Index listing each property with PropertyMetricsService::forLandlord metrics, each linking to properties.show; keep the buildings link for the building tier. Phase78 surface asserts the route renders the index.

findings.title: Persistent active property (users.active_property_id) + owner-gated switch
findings.evidence: There is no persistent active property; DashboardService just picks the first property when no ?property_id. A landlord with several properties has no 'current property' that sticks.
findings.remediation: Migration add users.active_property_id (nullable FK to properties, nullOnDelete). PropertyController::switch(Property) (or a dedicated controller) validates property.landlord_id === acting landlord, sets users.active_property_id, redirects back. App\Services\Property\ActivePropertyResolver::resolve(User): the stored property if still owned + present, else the landlord's first property, else null. Phase78 test: switch persists + resolver returns it; switching to another landlord's property is rejected; a deleted active property falls back.

findings.title: Nav property switcher
findings.evidence: AuthenticatedLayout has a Buildings link but no property dropdown; switching properties is invisible.
findings.remediation: A property switcher in AuthenticatedLayout (landlord only): a dropdown of the landlord's properties (shared via HandleInertiaRequests or a layout prop) showing the active one, POSTing to the switch route. data-testid='property-switcher'. lang nav.property_switcher.*. Phase78 surface asserts the component + the shared prop.

findings.title: Property-scoped views default to the active property
findings.evidence: Without a default, property show + dashboard need an explicit id every time.
findings.remediation: PropertyController::show with no id (a properties.current route) + the dashboard property selection fall back to ActivePropertyResolver when no explicit ?property_id. Switching the active property changes what these render. Phase78 test: the current-property route renders the resolved active property.

findings.title: PropertyBenchmarkService — per-property occupancy / NOI-margin / gross-yield + percentile rank
findings.evidence: NoiService::byProperty gives raw per-property NOI but no comparison; a landlord can't see which property under/over-performs.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Property\PropertyBenchmarkService::forLandlord: join PropertyMetricsService::forLandlord (occupancy) + NoiService::byProperty (revenue/NOI/margin) per property, compute gross_yield (annualised NOI / estimated_value, null when no value) + a percentile rank per metric across the portfolio + the portfolio median. Strictly landlord-scoped; reuses the two services (no duplicate SQL). Phase78 test: ranks order properties by occupancy + margin; single-property portfolio degrades gracefully; cross-tenant isolation.

findings.title: Benchmark surface (landlord report/dashboard section)
findings.remediation: A property-benchmark surface (a landlord route + Vue, or a section on the property index) rendering the per-property occupancy/margin/yield table with rank badges + the portfolio median. Route properties.benchmark (role:landlord). data-testid='property-benchmark'. lang property.benchmark.*. Phase78 test: route is landlord-gated + payload carries the ranked rows.

findings.title: Portfolio occupancy gauge from a rollup
findings.evidence: No metric tracks portfolio occupancy over time.
findings.remediation: Extend an existing daily rollup (or a small property:rollup cron) to emit property_portfolio_occupancy_pct{landlord_id} (top-50 landlords, bounded) from PropertyMetricsService. Gauge name follows metrics-naming.md. Phase78 test asserts the gauge name present in the emitter.

findings.title: Phase78PropertyDepthSurfaceTest cross-category presence map
findings.evidence: Each category needs a locked assertion a refactor cannot silently regress.
findings.remediation: Phase78PropertyDepthSurfaceTest: building_amenity_details + users.active_property_id schema present; BuildingAmenityDetail + AmenityDetailService + PropertyMetricsService + ActivePropertyResolver + PropertyBenchmarkService classes exist; routes properties.show/properties.benchmark/property switch registered; Property/Show.vue + property-switcher tokens exist; lang property.* parity en/sw/ar; portfolio-occupancy gauge present; runbook mentions Phase 78.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/property.md (NEW)
findings.evidence: No runbook documents the property tier, the active-property model, amenity details, or the benchmark.
findings.remediation: NEW docs/runbooks/property.md: Property→Building→Unit model, amenity-detail storage + allow-list gating, the active-property resolver + switcher, PropertyMetricsService + NoiService + PropertyBenchmarkService (occupancy/margin/yield/percentile), the portfolio gauge + incident playbook. Phase78 surface asserts the file + Phase 78 mention.

findings.title: Closeout + nav entry
findings.evidence: The property index/benchmark need nav reachability; the cycle needs its closeout block.
findings.remediation: Nav links to properties.index + properties.benchmark (landlord); scripts/phase78-closeout.mjs sets passes:true + closeout block. Phase78 surface stays green.
